frameworks.name: variable-precision continuous resource
frameworks.one_line: memory resource is continuous and fluctuates randomly in encoding precision across items and trials

frameworks.name: slot model of visual short-term memory
frameworks.one_line: fixed number of discrete memory slots producing guessing in responses

frameworks.name: fixed continuous-resource model
frameworks.one_line: continuous resource spread evenly, refuted by apparent guessing

frameworks.name: doubly stochastic neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: variability in population gain proposed as neural correlate of precision fluctuations

core_question: Is the capacity limit of visual short-term memory better explained by discrete slots or by a continuous resource whose encoding precision varies across items and trials?
key_assumptions: memory resource is shared across all stored items
encoding precision varies randomly across items and trials
apparent guessing can emerge from low-precision tail of the distribution
key_findings: variable-precision model accounts for all aspects of the behavioural data including apparent guessing
model outperforms slot models in formal model comparison
interpretations: VSTM resource is continuous and variable rather than discrete and fixed
precision variability may correspond to neural population gain variability
